name: piwheels
vendor: piwheels project (created/maintained by Ben Nuttall; infrastructure sponsored by Mythic Beasts; origins at the Raspberry Pi Foundation)
cost: Free. The public piwheels.org service is free and anonymous; the software is open source (BSD-3-Clause) and self-hostable at no cost.
language: Python (with PL/pgSQL for the PostgreSQL schema, plus a little JS/CSS for the web pages)
last_updated: 2025-07-09 (v0.22 release, latest tag as of 2026-06); public service continuously building, with cp313 wheels present as of 2026 (Raspberry Pi OS Trixie)
perf_notes: Detects new PyPI releases within ~10 seconds; per-package build time ranges from seconds (pure Python) to several hours (large C/C++ extensions like scipy). Serving pre-built ARM wheels turns multi-minute on-Pi source compiles (numpy/scipy/pandas/cryptography) into second-scale installs. The simple index and wheel files are static and served by a front-end web server; master/slave comms use ZeroMQ (semi-async DEALER socket, FETCH/CHUNK chunked file transfer) for performance.
scale_notes: ZeroMQ master/slave build farm: a single piw-master (monolith of threaded tasks: a DB juggler, a File Juggler for the served filesystem, and a slave/monitor handler) orchestrates a horizontally scalable cluster of Raspberry Pi 3/4 build slaves; design explicitly allows the DB and file-server tasks to be split onto separate machines. Production runs the web/files on one Raspberry Pi 4, PostgreSQL on a separate VM, and several Pis across OS versions as builders. Current scale (mid-2026): ~708k packages, ~7.84M wheels built, ~889M all-time downloads (~12.8M/30 days). No HA for the single master; throughput scales by adding slaves.
summary: Open-source build farm and package repository that auto-compiles ARM (armv6/armv7) wheels for every PyPI source distribution and serves them as a free, anonymous PEP 503 simple index to make pip install fast on the Raspberry Pi.
extras: Native ARM build farm: compiles wheels on real Raspberry Pi 3/4 hardware (no cross-compilation) for armv6l and armv7l; 64-bit aarch64 wheels are still not provided (a stated future goal, per the current FAQ)
Tracks the entire PyPI release stream and auto-builds wheels for any project that ships an sdist, within ~10s of release
Per-Python-version/OS build matrix (e.g. Buster cp37, Bullseye cp39, Bookworm cp311, Trixie cp313)
Pre-configured by default in Raspberry Pi OS via /etc/pip.conf extra-index-url
Per-project web pages showing versions, files, sizes, sha256, build status and links to full build logs (metadata pages, not rendered READMEs)
Public download statistics (all-time and 30-day) plus a stats blog and ClickHouse/Jupyter analysis

Custom (non-PEP-691) JSON API at /project/<name>/json (with per-version released time, yanked flag, prerelease, skip reasons, per-file filehash/size/abi/apt deps) and aggregate stats at /packages.json
Admin/operator CLI suite: piw-master, piw-slave, piw-monitor, piw-sense, piw-logger, piw-initdb, piw-import, piw-rebuild, piw-add, piw-remove

Repo ships a Dockerfile + docker-compose.yml and systemd unit files; deployment is configured via an INI file (piwheels.conf) and CLI args, not environment variables. No officially published/registry image
ZeroMQ-based master/slave protocol with chunked (FETCH/CHUNK) file transfer; the File Juggler verifies each transfer against its expected hash before atomically renaming it into place
Detects and skips known-unbuildable/abi-incompatible packages; mirrors PyPI yank status onto project pages and the JSON API
